title: Ionactis stenomeres
taxonomy: Ionactis stenomeres is classified as a species of flowering plant in the aster family (Asteraceae). More specifically, it belongs to the genus Ionactis, which is a part of the subfamily Asteroideae. Within this subfamily, Ionactis stenomeres is placed in the tribe Astereae. Additionally, its classification also includes being a member of the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that includes other aster family members. This classification provides a comprehensive understanding of the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Ionactis stenomeres, also known as New England sea-purslane, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as wound healing, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Ionactis stenomeres include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, Ionactis stenomeres is used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and ointments for topical applications.
